title: New ClawHub skill: agent-sentinel — vet a skill before you trust it
content:
Before you install a third-party OpenClaw/Hermes skill, ask one question: does this 'weather' skill really need shell access? It probably doesn't — and that's the #1 way agents go rogue.

So we built agent-sentinel and published it free on ClawHub: a stdlib-only, offline scanner that flags risky permission patterns in a skill folder before you give it machine access.

What it catches:
- simple-named skill requesting shell/exec -> HIGH
- any shell/exec request -> MEDIUM
- hardcoded secrets (api_key/token) in skill files -> HIGH
- no human approval gate for privileged actions -> LOW

Run it locally, nothing uploaded, no telemetry. MIT licensed.

ClawHub: clawhub.ai/skills/skills/agent-sentinel
GitHub: github.com/itsPremkumar/ai-product-packs

Free to use. If it saves you from a bad skill, sponsoring the builder is appreciated (GitHub Sponsors / Buy Me a Coffee links in the repo).
